name: Prof. Pramoda Kumar
bio: Prof. Kumar leads the Soft Matter Lab, focusing on topological defects in active nematics and phase transitions in liquid crystals.

name: Shri Gowri
bio: She completed her Bachelor’s degree at Mount Carmel College (MCC), Bangalore, followed by a Master’s in Physics from the University of Mysore, with a specialization in Solid State Physics.  Shri’s current research is curiosity-driven and focuses on understanding the collective dynamics of Caenorhabditis elegans. She approaches these organisms as a nematic, flexible active system due to their shape anisotropy and bidirectional motion-features that are characteristic of many other biological systems. Her work combines microscopy based experiments with computer vision and data driven modeling, employing deep learning tools such as U-Net for quantitative image analysis. Outside the lab, she enjoys cooking, reading, traveling, and engaging in creative activities such as building with Legos, playing board games, and designing model houses.
